mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art performance for large language models across various hardware accelerators. It provides intelligent scheduling, disaggregated serving, and expert-parallelism to efficiently handle real-world traffic. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate resources to leverage its full capabilities.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a managed Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --all

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step involves draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.6.0.yaml

kubectl get pods -l app=llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name to drain.
> This command upgrades the llm-d deployment to version 0.6.0.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the image is not available or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure that your Kubernetes cluster has access to the image repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the correct roles assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old scheduling methods in favor of the new intelligent inference scheduling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, ensure to update your configuration files to match the new schema.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing all llm-d pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
